summary: Iteration 257 — Verified the production fix for the two Zoho bank-transactions sync bugs: (a) zoho_service.fetch_bank_transactions() now sends `status=All` and NO `filter_by` param (the previous `filter_by=Status.All` was returning HTTP 400 from /banktransactions); (b) _run_sync() now enforces the requested [date_start,date_end] window client-side via _date_in_range(), so out-of-month rows are dropped even when Zoho ignores the date filter. Wrote a full pytest suite covering all 4 review-request areas. 12/12 tests PASS.
action_items: None — the production fix is verified end-to-end in this preview env. Safe to redeploy. After redeploy, run a real Zoho sync for the affected month and confirm (1) no 400 from /banktransactions and (2) only the chosen month's rows appear in the inbox.
critical_code_review_comments: services/zoho_service.py:3101-3128 — fetch_bank_transactions correctly defaults status='All' and only injects it when truthy; filter_by is fully removed. Comment block correctly documents WHY (Zoho 400 + date filter unreliable). Good defensive design.

routes/accounting_transactions.py:131-140 — _date_in_range is a tiny lexicographic-compare helper; inclusive on both ends, treats empty/None date as out-of-range (correct: rows with no date are unsafe to attribute to a month).

routes/accounting_transactions.py:200-204 — client-side window filter is applied INSIDE the page loop BEFORE the upsert build, so out-of-range rows never reach the DB. Also correctly gated by `if (date_start or date_end)` so a no-window sync still pulls everything.

routes/accounting_transactions.py:275-299 — sync error handler returns a sanitized, user-actionable error string (no stack-trace leak); special-cases the Zoho 401+code:57 banking-scope error with reconnect instructions. Solid.

Minor (non-blocking, not fixed): _run_sync's per-page `existing` lookup uses an `async for` over a find cursor — fine for ~200 rows/page, but a single `.to_list()` would be more idiomatic. Skip unless the per-page size grows.
test_credentials: CEO: surya.yadavalli@nylaairwater.earth / test123 (login returns session_token → Authorization: Bearer <session_token>) — same as iteration 256.
seed_data_creation: Integration test inserts ~3 rows into accounting_transactions and 1 row into accounting_txn_sync_jobs under a uuid-suffixed TEST_tenant_* tenant; teardown deletes all of them plus the per-tenant counter. Inbox not polluted.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Zoho is NOT connected in this preview tenant. All Zoho calls (fetch_bank_transactions, _zoho_request, get_credentials) are monkeypatched in tests. The integration test uses a UNIQUE TEST_tenant_* per run and cleans up in finally. To validate against live Zoho after redeploy, run a real /sync with a date_start/date_end and verify the job ends 'completed' with new>0 and that every row in the inbox falls inside the requested window.
rca of the issue: Production sync was failing with HTTP 400 because Zoho's /books/v3/banktransactions endpoint does NOT accept the `filter_by` query param (that param is used on /invoices, /customerpayments, etc., not banking). The correct param is `status` (values: All, uncategorized, categorized, matched, manually_added, excluded). Fix #1: change the param name + value. Fix #2: even with status=All accepted, Zoho frequently ignores date_start/date_end for /banktransactions and returns rows outside the requested window — so the requested month was never honoured. _run_sync now re-applies the window client-side via _date_in_range (inclusive lex compare on YYYY-MM-DD strings). Both fixes verified via unit + integration tests; regression endpoints (POST /sync 400 when not connected, GET list/flow-summary/export 2xx) all green.
